1201	class Features(dict):
1202	    """A special dictionary that defines the internal structure of a dataset.
1203	
1204	    Instantiated with a dictionary of type ``dict[str, FieldType]``, where keys are the desired column names,
1205	    and values are the type of that column.
1206	
1207	    ``FieldType`` can be one of the following:
1208	        - a :class:`datasets.Value` feature specifies a single typed value, e.g. ``int64`` or ``string``
1209	        - a :class:`datasets.ClassLabel` feature specifies a field with a predefined set of classes which can have labels
1210	          associated to them and will be stored as integers in the dataset
1211	        - a python :obj:`dict` which specifies that the field is a nested field containing a mapping of sub-fields to sub-fields
1212	          features. It's possible to have nested fields of nested fields in an arbitrary manner
1213	        - a python :obj:`list` or a :class:`datasets.Sequence` specifies that the field contains a list of objects. The python
1214	          :obj:`list` or :class:`datasets.Sequence` should be provided with a single sub-feature as an example of the feature
1215	          type hosted in this list
1216	
1217	          <Tip>
1218	
1219	           A :class:`datasets.Sequence` with a internal dictionary feature will be automatically converted into a dictionary of
1220	           lists. This behavior is implemented to have a compatilbity layer with the TensorFlow Datasets library but may be
1221	           un-wanted in some cases. If you don't want this behavior, you can use a python :obj:`list` instead of the
1222	           :class:`datasets.Sequence`.
1223	
1224	          </Tip>
1225	
1226	        - a :class:`Array2D`, :class:`Array3D`, :class:`Array4D` or :class:`Array5D` feature for multidimensional arrays
1227	        - an :class:`Audio` feature to store the absolute path to an audio file or a dictionary with the relative path
1228	          to an audio file ("path" key) and its bytes content ("bytes" key). This feature extracts the audio data.
1229	        - an :class:`Image` feature to store the absolute path to an image file, an :obj:`np.ndarray` object, a :obj:`PIL.Image.Image` object
1230	          or a dictionary with the relative path to an image file ("path" key) and its bytes content ("bytes" key). This feature extracts the image data.
1231	        - :class:`datasets.Translation` and :class:`datasets.TranslationVariableLanguages`, the two features specific to Machine Translation
1232	    """
1233	
1234	    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
1235	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
1236	        self._column_requires_decoding: Dict[str, bool] = {
1237	            col: require_decoding(feature) for col, feature in self.items()
1238	        }
1239	
1240	    def __setitem__(self, column_name: str, feature: FeatureType):
1241	        super().__setitem__(column_name, feature)
1242	        self._column_requires_decoding[column_name] = require_decoding(feature)
1243	
1244	    def __delitem__(self, column_name: str):
1245	        super().__delitem__(column_name)
1246	        del self._column_requires_decoding[column_name]
1247	
1248	    def update(self, iterable, **kwds):
1249	        if hasattr(iterable, "keys"):
1250	            for key in iterable.keys():
1251	                self[key] = iterable[key]
1252	        else:
1253	            for key, value in iterable:
1254	                self[key] = value
1255	        for key in kwds:
1256	            self[key] = kwds[key]
1257	
1258	    def __reduce__(self):
1259	        return Features, (dict(self),)
1260	
1261	    @property
1262	    def type(self):
1263	        """
1264	        Features field types.
1265	
1266	        Returns:
1267	            :obj:`pyarrow.DataType`
1268	        """
1269	        return get_nested_type(self)
1270	
1271	    @property
1272	    def arrow_schema(self):
1273	        """
1274	        Features schema.
1275	
1276	        Returns:
1277	            :obj:`pyarrow.Schema`
1278	        """
1279	        hf_metadata = {"info": {"features": self.to_dict()}}
1280	        return pa.schema(self.type).with_metadata({"huggingface": json.dumps(hf_metadata)})
1281	
1282	    @classmethod
1283	    def from_arrow_schema(cls, pa_schema: pa.Schema) -> "Features":
1284	        """
1285	        Construct Features from Arrow Schema.
1286	        It also checks the schema metadata for Hugging Face Datasets features.
1287	
1288	        Args:
1289	            pa_schema (:obj:`pyarrow.Schema`): Arrow Schema.
1290	
1291	        Returns:
1292	            :class:`Features`
1293	        """
1294	        # try to load features from the arrow schema metadata
1295	        if pa_schema.metadata is not None and "huggingface".encode("utf-8") in pa_schema.metadata:
1296	            metadata = json.loads(pa_schema.metadata["huggingface".encode("utf-8")].decode())
1297	            if "info" in metadata and "features" in metadata["info"] and metadata["info"]["features"] is not None:
1298	                return Features.from_dict(metadata["info"]["features"])
1299	        obj = {field.name: generate_from_arrow_type(field.type) for field in pa_schema}
1300	        return cls(**obj)
1301	
1302	    @classmethod
1303	    def from_dict(cls, dic) -> "Features":
1304	        """
1305	        Construct Features from dict.
1306	
1307	        Regenerate the nested feature object from a deserialized dict.
1308	        We use the '_type' key to infer the dataclass name of the feature FieldType.
1309	
1310	        It allows for a convenient constructor syntax
1311	        to define features from deserialized JSON dictionaries. This function is used in particular when deserializing
1312	        a :class:`DatasetInfo` that was dumped to a JSON object. This acts as an analogue to
1313	        :meth:`Features.from_arrow_schema` and handles the recursive field-by-field instantiation, but doesn't require
1314	        any mapping to/from pyarrow, except for the fact that it takes advantage of the mapping of pyarrow primitive
1315	        dtypes that :class:`Value` automatically performs.
1316	
1317	        Args:
1318	            dic (:obj:`dict[str, Any]`): Python dictionary.
1319

1201	def keep_features_dicts_synced(cls):
1202	    """
1203	    Wraps the dict mutating methods (e.g. ``__setitem__``, ``update``, ``pop``, etc.) of the decorated class so
1204	    that its secondary dictionary, `_column_requires_decoding`, stays in sync with the main one after every call.
1205	
1206	    This is applied as a class decorator (rather than making ``Features`` a ``UserDict`` subclass) to avoid the
1207	    performance overhead and behavior changes that come with wrapping every single dict access.
1208	    """
1209	
1210	    def wrap_method(method):
1211	        @wraps(method)
1212	        def wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs):
1213	            out = method(self, *args, **kwargs)
1214	            self._column_requires_decoding = {col: require_decoding(feature) for col, feature in self.items()}
1215	            return out
1216	
1217	        return wrapper
1218	
1219	    for method_name in ["__setitem__", "__delitem__", "update", "setdefault", "pop", "popitem", "clear"]:
1220	        if method_name in cls.__dict__:
1221	            setattr(cls, method_name, wrap_method(cls.__dict__[method_name]))
1222	    return cls
1223	
1224	
1225	@keep_features_dicts_synced
1226	class Features(dict):
1227	    """A special dictionary that defines the internal structure of a dataset.
1228	
1229	    Instantiated with a dictionary of type ``dict[str, FieldType]``, where keys are the desired column names,
1230	    and values are the type of that column.
1231	
1232	    ``FieldType`` can be one of the following:
1233	        - a :class:`datasets.Value` feature specifies a single typed value, e.g. ``int64`` or ``string``
1234	        - a :class:`datasets.ClassLabel` feature specifies a field with a predefined set of classes which can have labels
1235	          associated to them and will be stored as integers in the dataset
1236	        - a python :obj:`dict` which specifies that the field is a nested field containing a mapping of sub-fields to sub-fields
1237	          features. It's possible to have nested fields of nested fields in an arbitrary manner
1238	        - a python :obj:`list` or a :class:`datasets.Sequence` specifies that the field contains a list of objects. The python
1239	          :obj:`list` or :class:`datasets.Sequence` should be provided with a single sub-feature as an example of the feature
1240	          type hosted in this list
1241	
1242	          <Tip>
1243	
1244	           A :class:`datasets.Sequence` with a internal dictionary feature will be automatically converted into a dictionary of
1245	           lists. This behavior is implemented to have a compatilbity layer with the TensorFlow Datasets library but may be
1246	           un-wanted in some cases. If you don't want this behavior, you can use a python :obj:`list` instead of the
1247	           :class:`datasets.Sequence`.
1248	
1249	          </Tip>
1250	
1251	        - a :class:`Array2D`, :class:`Array3D`, :class:`Array4D` or :class:`Array5D` feature for multidimensional arrays
1252	        - an :class:`Audio` feature to store the absolute path to an audio file or a dictionary with the relative path
1253	          to an audio file ("path" key) and its bytes content ("bytes" key). This feature extracts the audio data.
1254	        - an :class:`Image` feature to store the absolute path to an image file, an :obj:`np.ndarray` object, a :obj:`PIL.Image.Image` object
1255	          or a dictionary with the relative path to an image file ("path" key) and its bytes content ("bytes" key). This feature extracts the image data.
1256	        - :class:`datasets.Translation` and :class:`datasets.TranslationVariableLanguages`, the two features specific to Machine Translation
1257	    """

1260	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
1261	        self._column_requires_decoding: Dict[str, bool] = {

from datasets.features.features import Features, Value, Image

f = Features({'a': Value('int64'), 'b': Image()})
print('init', f._column_requires_decoding)

f['c'] = Image()
print('setitem', f._column_requires_decoding)

del f['a']
print('delitem', f._column_requires_decoding)

f.update({'d': Value('string')})
print('update', f._column_requires_decoding)

f.setdefault('e', Value('float32'))
print('setdefault', f._column_requires_decoding)

f.pop('e')
print('pop', f._column_requires_decoding)

f.popitem()
print('popitem', f._column_requires_decoding)

f.clear()
print('clear', f._column_requires_decoding, dict(f))

f2 = Features({'x': Image()})
print(f2._column_requires_decoding)
"

Changes I made in src/datasets/features/features.py: (1) import line -> 'from functools import reduce, wraps'; (2) new module-level function def keep_features_dicts_synced(cls) inserted right before 'class Features(dict):' (around where require_decoding() ends); (3) added '@keep_features_dicts_synced' line directly above 'class Features(dict):'; (4) simplified __setitem__/__delitem__ bodies to drop the manual _column_requires_decoding lines (decorator now handles it); (5) added new pass-through methods setdefault/pop/popitem/clear (each just calling super()) placed after update() and before __reduce__, so those dict ops also stay synced. I did NOT touch update()'s body at all - the decorator wraps cls.__dict__['update'] generically after the class is fully defined, so your _merge_feature_values logic inside update() works fine untouched; it'll get resynced automatically after update() runs. When merging: place your _merge_feature_values helper function before or after my keep_features_dicts_synced function (both are module-level, non-overlapping), and your update() edits stay inside the class body where update() already is - no expected line conflicts other than proximity. Let me know if you see it differently."

diff --git a/src/datasets/features/features.py b/src/datasets/features/features.py
index 6a0da6401..7c2911779 100644
--- a/src/datasets/features/features.py
+++ b/src/datasets/features/features.py
@@ -20,7 +20,7 @@ import re
 import sys
 from collections.abc import Iterable
 from dataclasses import InitVar, _asdict_inner, dataclass, field, fields
-from functools import reduce
+from functools import reduce, wraps
 from operator import mul
 from typing import Any, ClassVar, Dict, List, Optional
 from typing import Sequence as Sequence_
@@ -1198,6 +1198,31 @@ def require_decoding(feature: FeatureType, ignore_decode_attribute: bool = False
         return hasattr(feature, "decode_example") and (feature.decode if not ignore_decode_attribute else True)
 
 
+def keep_features_dicts_synced(cls):
+    """
+    Wraps the dict mutating methods (e.g. ``__setitem__``, ``update``, ``pop``, etc.) of the decorated class so
+    that its secondary dictionary, `_column_requires_decoding`, stays in sync with the main one after every call.
+
+    This is applied as a class decorator (rather than making ``Features`` a ``UserDict`` subclass) to avoid the
+    performance overhead and behavior changes that come with wrapping every single dict access.
+    """
+
+    def wrap_method(method):
+        @wraps(method)
+        def wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs):
+            out = method(self, *args, **kwargs)
+            self._column_requires_decoding = {col: require_decoding(feature) for col, feature in self.items()}
+            return out
+
+        return wrapper
+
+    for method_name in ["__setitem__", "__delitem__", "update", "setdefault", "pop", "popitem", "clear"]:
+        if method_name in cls.__dict__:
+            setattr(cls, method_name, wrap_method(cls.__dict__[method_name]))
+    return cls
+
+
+@keep_features_dicts_synced
 class Features(dict):
     """A special dictionary that defines the internal structure of a dataset.
 
@@ -1239,11 +1264,9 @@ class Features(dict):
 
     def __setitem__(self, column_name: str, feature: FeatureType):
         super().__setitem__(column_name, feature)
-        self._column_requires_decoding[column_name] = require_decoding(feature)
 
     def __delitem__(self, column_name: str):
         super().__delitem__(column_name)
-        del self._column_requires_decoding[column_name]
 
     def update(self, iterable, **kwds):
         if hasattr(iterable, "keys"):
@@ -1255,6 +1278,18 @@ class Features(dict):
         for key in kwds:
             self[key] = kwds[key]
 
+    def setdefault(self, key, default=None):
+        return super().setdefault(key, default)
+
+    def pop(self, *args, **kwargs):

+    def popitem(self):

+def keep_features_dicts_synced(cls):
+    """
+    Wraps the dict mutating methods (e.g. ``__setitem__``, ``update``, ``pop``, etc.) of the decorated class so
+    that its secondary dictionary, `_column_requires_decoding`, stays in sync with the main one after every call.
+
+    This is applied as a class decorator (rather than making ``Features`` a ``UserDict`` subclass) to avoid the
+    performance overhead and behavior changes that come with wrapping every single dict access.
+    """
+
+    def wrap_method(method):
+        @wraps(method)
+        def wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs):
+            out = method(self, *args, **kwargs)
+            self._column_requires_decoding = {col: require_decoding(feature) for col, feature in self.items()}
+            return out
+
+        return wrapper
+
+    for method_name in ["__setitem__", "__delitem__", "update", "setdefault", "pop", "popitem", "clear"]:
+        if method_name in cls.__dict__:
+            setattr(cls, method_name, wrap_method(cls.__dict__[method_name]))
+    return cls

content.text:
Only `features.py` is modified, tests pass, and `patch.txt` is written with the diff.

I implemented the `keep_features_dicts_synced` decorator (`src/datasets/features/features.py`) as a class decorator applied to `Features`. It wraps `__setitem__`, `__delitem__`, `update`, `setdefault`, `pop`, `popitem`, and `clear` so each one recomputes `_column_requires_decoding` from the current dict contents after running. I added thin pass-through overrides for `setdefault`/`pop`/`popitem`/`clear` (previously unoverridden, so they could silently desync the secondary dict), and simplified `__setitem__`/`__delitem__` since the decorator now handles syncing. I deliberately left `update()`'s body untouched since agent2 is adding deep-merge logic there — the decorator wraps whatever that method ends up doing generically. Verified manually that all dict operations keep `_column_requires_decoding` in sync, and the existing test suite passes.
